did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a review that summarizes the application of AI, machine learning, and deep learning methods for skin cancer diagnostics. It explicitly discusses and lists specific AI models and their effectiveness metrics for skin cancer classification tasks, including MLP (97.13%), Naive Bayes (92.13%), LMTtree (95.97%), MSI-CAD (92.0%), Derm-CAD (90.1%), Beyes Net (85.97%), Inception V3 (85.8%), Inception Resnet (84%), Resnet50 (83.7%), and CNN (83.2%). It also mentions neural network classifiers for automated skin cancer diagnosis.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper is explicitly labeled as a 'REVIEW' in the header and the abstract states 'This paper is a review paper'. It summarizes current solutions and literature regarding image processing methods for skin cancer diagnostics from 2017 to 2022, rather than presenting original experimental results.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
